raw_output: {"predictions":[{"common_name":"House Sparrow","scientific_name":"Passer domesticus","rank_hint":"species","confidence":0.95,"field_marks":"streaked brown upperparts, gray crown, chestnut nape, black bib (male), small conical bill, foraging near human feet"},{"common_name":"Spanish Sparrow","scientific_name":"Passer hispaniolensis","rank_hint":"species","confidence":0.3,"field_marks":"similar to House Sparrow but bolder head pattern, less common in urban settings outside Mediterranean region"},{"common_name":"Sparrow","scientific_name":"Passer spp.","rank_hint":"genus","confidence":0.1,"field_marks":"small passerine, streaked brown plumage, ground-foraging behavior"},{"common_name":"Tree Sparrow","scientific_name":"Passer montanus","rank_hint":"species","confidence":0.05,"field_marks":"distinctive black cheek spot and chestnut crown, less associated with dense urban foot traffic than House Sparrow"},{"common_name":"Old World Sparrow","scientific_name":"Passeridae","rank_hint":"family","confidence":0.02,"field_marks":"small, stout-billed seed-eaters, typically brown-streaked plumage"}],"abstain":false,"abstain_reason":null,"overall_confidence":0.95}
